comment: Synthetic recurrent / metastatic HNSCC, male 59, hypopharyngeal SCC, HPV-negative, current heavy smoker + heavy alcohol use, ECOG 1, lung + bone metastases at presentation, PD-L1 CPS 0 (negative). CLINICAL TARGET: EXTREME regimen archetype — cetuximab + cisplatin + 5-FU per Vermorken NEJM 2008 (mOS 10.1 mo vs 7.4 mo platin+5FU alone). KB-DRIFT NOTE (most prominent of the chunk): no IND-HNSCC-RM-1L-EXTREME exists in the KB. ALGO-HNSCC-RM-1L explicitly notes: 'CPS <1 R/M HNSCC ideally routes to EXTREME (cetuximab + 5-FU + platinum) or chemo-IO with attenuated OS benefit; explicit IND-HNSCC-RM-1L-EXTREME is deferred to a follow-up content session.' Engine therefore returns default IND-HNSCC-RM-1L-PEMBRO-CHEMO regardless of CPS<1 — the CPS-stratified branch is not authored, and chemo-IO with attenuated benefit is the surrogate the engine prints. The IND IS contraindicated for this CPS<1 profile per evidence (HR for OS in CPS<1 subset of KEYNOTE-048 chemo-IO arm crossed 1.0). Tumor board must be aware that the rendered Plan is the closest-available KB recommendation, NOT the actual standard for CPS<1 — clinician judgment required to substitute EXTREME or another cetuximab-based platinum doublet. Flagged for KB authors as the highest-priority missing Indication in HNSCC.
